description_long: A collection of brown cardboard parcels is stacked together, with some boxes arranged in a neat pile and others positioned separately. The parcels vary slightly in size but are all rectangular, featuring visible seams and printed labels or markings, including black triangular symbols and white address labels. One box is open, revealing a dark, empty interior, while the rest are closed and appear to be sealed for shipping. The cardboard surfaces have a matte, slightly rough texture typical of corrugated packaging. These parcels are commonly seen in urban environments such as delivery zones, warehouses, or on sidewalks awaiting pickup. The overall appearance suggests recent delivery or preparation for transport.
description: A group of brown cardboard parcels, some stacked and one open, with visible labels and shipping markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing a stack of four parcels, one open box above, and a single parcel to the left.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the stacked arrangement and the open box on top, with a single parcel set apart to the right.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the stacked parcels, the open box on top, and a single parcel to the right.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the vertical stack of parcels and the open box, with a single parcel to the left.
